tasks.description: Automatically retry failed tool calls using LLM-guided correction.
tasks.details: Capture tool errors, feed them back into the LLM, request a corrected tool call, and retry. Add retry limits and error classification. Integrate with the task graph engine for multi-step correction.
tasks.testStrategy: Force a file_read error and verify that the system retries with corrected arguments.
tasks.title: Implement Self-Correction Loop

tasks.description: Load project-specific knowledge files into context automatically.
tasks.details: Create a `knowledge/` directory. Load `.md` and `.json` files. Add relevance scoring and context injection rules. Expose a command to reload knowledge packs.
tasks.notes: Wired: KnowledgeLoader.get_all() added; initialize_session now calls KnowledgeLoader::load() and injects all knowledge/*.md+*.json content as a system message at session start.
tasks.testStrategy: Place a markdown file in `knowledge/` and verify that the LLM references it when relevant.
tasks.title: Add Local Knowledge Pack Loader

tasks.description: Add a persistent personality and behavior configuration file.
tasks.details: Create a `session_dna.json` file defining tone, verbosity, risk tolerance, coding style, and tool preferences. Load it at session start and inject into system prompts.
tasks.notes: Wired: initialize_session now calls SessionDna::load() and appends its fields (tone, verbosity) to the system prompt at session startup. Existing system messages are extended; a new one is created if none exists.
tasks.testStrategy: Modify tone settings and verify that LLM responses change accordingly.

tasks.description: Allow users to drop Rust files into a folder to create new tools.

tasks.description: Implement a dry-run mode where tools do not execute but return simulated results.
tasks.details: Add a `--simulate` flag. Wrap tool calls in a simulation layer that returns mock outputs. Ensure no file writes or shell commands execute. Integrate with task graph engine.
tasks.notes: Fully implemented: src/agent/simulator.rs (SimulationResult, parse_simulation_response, display_simulation_result, SIMULATION_SYSTEM_PROMPT) wired into src/display/interactive.rs with /simulate on|off|status commands, run_simulation() async fn, and simulate_mode field on InteractiveSession. All tests pass. Not yet available in the ACP/Zed path, only the standalone interactive CLI.

tasks.description: Allow users to set persistent goals that influence interpretation of all messages.

tasks.description: Generate a visual graph of the Grok-CLI routing and execution pipeline.

tasks.description: When conversation history approaches the model token limit, automatically summarize the oldest messages with the AI, persist them to the session archive on disk, replace them with a compact notice in the active context, and allow retrieval via /recall or a recall_context tool.
tasks.details:
## Problem
The current trimming strategy (count-based + token-budget) simply drops old messages when context fills up. This permanently loses potentially important conversation history. Users working on long coding sessions frequently lose earlier decisions, file edits, and reasoning chains.

## Goal
Implement a four-stage context lifecycle that replaces blind dropping with intelligent summarization:

1. DETECT — when estimated prompt tokens exceed `compression_threshold` (default 75%) of `max_context_tokens`, trigger compression.
2. SUMMARIZE — take the oldest `compression_chunk_ratio` (default 40%) of messages and make a fast API call to produce a bullet-point summary + key_facts list.
3. ARCHIVE — persist the full raw messages + summary to disk at `~/.grok/sessions/{session_id}/archives/chunk_NNN.json` and update an `index.json`.
4. REPLACE — remove the archived messages from `session.messages` and insert a compact system-role archive-notice message (~50 tokens) so the model knows the history exists and how to recall it.

## Archive Notice Format (injected into active context)
```
[Context Archive #N | {timestamp}]
Summary: {2-3 sentence AI summary of what was discussed/done}.
Key facts: {bullet list}.
Say "recall archive N" or type `/recall N` to restore this context.
```

## Storage Layout
```
~/.grok/sessions/{session_id}/
  episode.json          (existing — end-of-session metadata)
  transcript.json       (existing — full raw transcript)
  archives/
    index.json          (list of chunk IDs + token counts)
    chunk_001.json      (raw messages + AI summary)
    chunk_002.json
    ...
```

## New Rust Modules
- `src/memory/context_archive.rs` — `ContextChunk`, `ContextArchive` structs + atomic disk I/O.
- `src/memory/context_compressor.rs` — `ContextCompressor` that calls the Grok API to summarize a message batch.

## New Config Fields (AcpConfig in src/config/mod.rs)
```toml
# Master switch — disable to revert to old drop-only behaviour.
auto_compress = true
# Fraction of max_context_tokens at which compression fires (0.0–1.0).
compression_threshold = 0.75
# Fraction of current non-system messages compressed per event (0.0–1.0).
compression_chunk_ratio = 0.40
```

## Integration Point
Layer 4 is added to `handle_chat_completion` in `src/acp/mod.rs`, after the existing three layers (per-message truncation, count trim, token-budget trim).

## New Slash Commands (src/acp/slash_commands.rs)
- `/recall N` — retrieve chunk N back into active context as a system message.
- `/archives` — list all archived chunks for this session (ID, timestamp, token count, one-line summary).

## New Tool (src/tools/ or src/acp/tools.rs)
- `recall_context { chunk_id }` — allows the AI itself to pull an archived chunk back in when it detects it needs earlier information. Returns the summary + key_facts; the full raw messages are injected as a system message.

subtasks.details: Define ContextChunk { chunk_id: u32, session_id, created_at, message_count, estimated_tokens_saved, summary, key_facts: Vec<String>, raw_messages: Vec<Value> } and ContextArchive { session_id, sessions_dir, index: Vec<ChunkMeta> }. Implement save_chunk (atomic write-then-rename to chunk_NNN.json + update index.json), load_chunk, list_chunks, and session_archive_dir helper. Mirror the atomic_write pattern from EpisodicMemory.
subtasks.title: ContextChunk + ContextArchive types and disk I/O (src/memory/context_archive.rs)

subtasks.details: Implement async fn compress(messages: &[Value], router: &AppRouter, model: &str) -> Result<(String, Vec<String>)>. Build a one-shot prompt: 'Summarize the following conversation in 2-3 sentences and list up to 10 key facts as bullet points.' Call chat_completion_with_history with the batch as user content. Parse the response into (summary_text, key_facts). Apply Starlink-safe retries (3 attempts, 5s/10s/20s backoff). Keep the summarization call short-circuited if messages is empty.
subtasks.title: ContextCompressor — AI-powered batch summarizer (src/memory/context_compressor.rs)

subtasks.details: Add three fields to AcpConfig in src/config/mod.rs with serde defaults. Add matching default fns: default_auto_compress() -> bool = true, default_compression_threshold() -> f32 = 0.75, default_compression_chunk_ratio() -> f32 = 0.40. Update AcpConfig::default(). Document all three fields in config.example.toml and .grok/config.toml.
subtasks.title: New AcpConfig fields: auto_compress, compression_threshold, compression_chunk_ratio

subtasks.details: After the existing three-layer trim block, add Layer 4. Check: config.acp.auto_compress && estimated_tokens > threshold. If triggered: 1) Calculate how many non-system messages to compress (len * compression_chunk_ratio, minimum 4). 2) Drain those messages from session.messages (preserve system message at index 0). 3) Call ContextCompressor::compress. 4) Call ContextArchive::save_chunk. 5) Build the archive notice JSON value and insert at index 1 (right after system message). 6) Emit a warn!() log with chunk ID and tokens saved. If the summarization API call itself fails (network drop), fall back to the existing token-budget trim — never crash the session.
subtasks.title: Layer 4 compression trigger in handle_chat_completion (src/acp/mod.rs)
Archive notice message builder

subtasks.details: Add SlashCommand::Recall { chunk_id: Option<u32> } and SlashCommand::Archives. Parse '/recall' (list all) and '/recall N' (inject chunk N). In handle_builtin: for Archives — call ContextArchive::list_chunks and format as a markdown table (ID | Date | Messages | Tokens Saved | Summary preview). For Recall { Some(n) } — load chunk from disk, inject raw messages as a single merged system message prefixed with '[Recalled Archive #N]', return BuiltinResult::InjectMessages. For Recall { None } — same as Archives. Wire into parse_slash_command.
subtasks.title: /recall and /archives slash commands (src/acp/slash_commands.rs)

subtasks.details: Register a new tool definition recall_context with parameter chunk_id: u32 (required). When called during a tool loop in handle_chat_completion: load the chunk from ContextArchive, return its summary + key_facts as the tool result string, and append the raw messages to session.messages as a batch system message. This allows the AI to self-retrieve earlier context when it detects it needs information it can no longer see. Add to get_available_tool_definitions().
subtasks.title: recall_context AI tool (src/acp/tools.rs or src/tools/)

subtasks.details: Unit tests: ContextChunk serialisation round-trip, ContextArchive save + load, build_archive_notice format check, compression_chunk_ratio boundary cases (min 4 messages). Integration test: mock router that returns a fixed summary; run Layer 4 trigger; assert chunk file exists and session.messages contains the notice. Update CONFIGURATION.md with new ACP config fields. Update TROUBLESHOOTING.md: 'Why did my context get archived?' section. Add CHANGELOG entry. Run cargo clippy -D warnings and cargo test before marking done.
subtasks.title: Tests, docs, and CHANGELOG

tasks.testStrategy:
1. Start a long session that produces > 75% of max_context_tokens. Verify that chunk_001.json appears in ~/.grok/sessions/{id}/archives/ and that the active session.messages contains the archive notice instead of the original messages.
2. Type `/recall 1` — verify the archived context is injected back and the model can answer questions about it.
3. Use the `recall_context` tool from within an AI turn — verify it returns the summary correctly.
4. Run `/archives` — verify all chunk IDs + metadata are listed.
5. Set `auto_compress = false` — verify old drop-only behaviour is restored.
6. Simulate Starlink drop during the summarization API call — verify the archive write is atomic (no partial files) and the session continues without error.
tasks.title: Intelligent Auto Context Management

tasks.description: Update all model references, capabilities, and context-management logic to fully leverage grok-4.3's 1,000,000-token context window while keeping backward compatibility with grok-3 (256 k) and grok-2 (131 k).
tasks.details:
## Background
grok-4.3 is now the default model and exposes a 1,048,576-token context window — roughly 4× the grok-3 window.  The current code has several hardcoded or config-defaulted values that are tuned for grok-3 (256 k) and must be updated.

## Changes Required

### 1. src/acp/mod.rs — create_capabilities()
- Set `max_context_length: 1_048_576` (up from 131_072).
- Add `"grok-4.3"` as the first entry in `models` vec; keep grok-3, grok-3-mini, grok-2-vision-1212, grok-2 as fallbacks.
- Add feature flags: `"1m_context"`, `"vision"` (grok-4.3 is multimodal).

### 2. src/config/mod.rs — AcpConfig
- Add new field `grok4_max_context_tokens: usize` (default: 950_000).  This is the soft budget for grok-4.x models — leaves 50 k headroom for response + tool definitions.
- Rename/update the doc comment on `max_context_tokens` to clarify it applies to grok-3 and older.
- Add `fn default_grok4_max_context_tokens() -> usize { 950_000 }`.
- Update `AcpConfig::default()` to include the new field.

### 3. src/acp/mod.rs — handle_chat_completion (Layer 3 token-budget trim)
- Replace the single `max_ctx_tokens` lookup with model-aware selection:
```rust
let max_ctx_tokens = model_context_budget(
    &session.config.model,
    self.config.acp.max_context_tokens,
    self.config.acp.grok4_max_context_tokens,
);
```
- Add free function `fn model_context_budget(model: &str, grok3_budget: usize, grok4_budget: usize) -> usize` that returns grok4_budget when `model.starts_with("grok-4")`, else grok3_budget.
- Apply the same model-aware budget in Layer 4 (compression threshold calculation).

### 4. src/acp/mod.rs — SessionConfig::default()
- Keep model as `"grok-4.3"`.
- Raise `max_tokens` (output) to 16_384 — grok-4.3 supports higher output token limits.

### 5. src/config/mod.rs — Config defaults
- Update `default_max_tokens()` from 256_000 to 16_384 (this is the output-token budget; 256 k was erroneously set to match the input window).
- Update comment on `default_max_context_tokens()` to clarify 220_000 is the grok-3 budget.

### 6. config.example.toml + .grok/config.toml
- Add `grok4_max_context_tokens = 950000` under `[acp]`.
- Update comments to describe the model-selection logic.

### 7. CONFIGURATION.md
- Add a "Model Context Budgets" section documenting grok-4.3 vs grok-3 limits.

### 8. CHANGELOG.md
- Add entry for v0.2.0 noting grok-4.3 default and 1 M context support.

subtasks.title: Update create_capabilities — max_context_length = 1_048_576, models list
Add grok4_max_context_tokens field to AcpConfig + default fn
Add model_context_budget() helper and wire into Layer 3 + Layer 4
Fix default_max_tokens (output) from 256_000 to 16_384; update SessionConfig::default max_tokens
Update config.example.toml, CONFIGURATION.md, and CHANGELOG.md

tasks.testStrategy:
1. `cargo build --release` passes with zero warnings.
2. `cargo test` passes.
3. Run `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` — zero issues.
4. Start a session with grok-4.3: verify log line shows `max_ctx_tokens = 950000`.
5. Start a session with grok-3: verify log line shows `max_ctx_tokens = 220000`.
6. Confirm `GrokAgentCapabilities::max_context_length` equals 1_048_576 in a unit test.
7. Check `config.example.toml` contains `grok4_max_context_tokens`.
tasks.title: grok-4.3 Full Support — 1 Million Token Context Window

tasks.description: Add first-class support for grok-4.3 thinking/reasoning modes via the xAI API's reasoning_effort parameter.  Users can switch between Off / Low / High reasoning at runtime; thinking tokens are surfaced in the terminal UI and optionally persisted with session history.
tasks.details:
## Background
The xAI API exposes a `reasoning_effort` field on chat completion requests for models that support extended thinking (grok-3-mini, grok-4.x).  Valid values:
- `"none"` — standard response, no internal reasoning trace.
- `"low"` — light reasoning; faster and cheaper.
- `"high"` — deep reasoning; highest quality, slower, higher token cost.
The response may include a `thinking` or `reasoning_content` field in `choices[0].message` containing the model's internal chain-of-thought.

## Changes Required

### 1. src/config/mod.rs
- Add `ThinkingMode` enum:
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize, Default)]
#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
pub enum ThinkingMode {
    #[default]
    Off,    // reasoning_effort = "none" (not sent for non-reasoning models)
    Low,    // reasoning_effort = "low"
    High,   // reasoning_effort = "high"
}
```
- Add `thinking_mode: ThinkingMode` field to `AcpConfig` with `#[serde(default)]` and `default_thinking_mode` fn returning `ThinkingMode::Off`.

### 2. src/acp/mod.rs — SessionConfig
- Add `pub thinking_mode: ThinkingMode` field to `SessionConfig`.
- Update `SessionConfig::default()` to set `thinking_mode: ThinkingMode::Off`.

### 3. src/grok_client_ext.rs — GrokClient::chat_completion_with_history
- Add `reasoning_effort: Option<&str>` parameter (or `thinking_mode: Option<ThinkingMode>`).
- If `Some` and not `ThinkingMode::Off`, append `"reasoning_effort": "low"|"high"` to the JSON request body.
- Since grok_api v0.1.7's builder may not expose this field, inject it by either:
  (a) Adding a `.reasoning_effort(val)` builder method to the local grok_api crate (`../grok_crate/grok_api/src/client.rs`), or
  (b) Adding an `extra_body` field to the builder that merges a `serde_json::Map` into the request body before serialization.
- Preferred: option (a) — add `reasoning_effort: Option<String>` to the request builder in grok_api.

### 4. src/grok_client_ext.rs — MessageWithFinishReason + response parsing
- Extend `MessageWithFinishReason` with `pub thinking_content: Option<String>`.
- In `convert_response_to_message_with_finish_reason`: extract `choices[0].message.reasoning_content` or `choices[0].message.thinking` if present and store in `thinking_content`.
- Forward `thinking_content` back through `handle_chat_completion` → display layer.

### 5. src/acp/mod.rs — handle_chat_completion
- Pass `session.config.thinking_mode` (converted to `Option<&str>`) when calling `chat_completion_with_history`.
- After receiving the response, if `thinking_content` is Some, prepend it to the assistant message as a fenced block:
```
<details><summary>🧠 Thinking…</summary>

{thinking_content}

</details>
```
- Emit a `debug!()` log with token count of thinking content (len / 4).

### 6. src/acp/slash_commands.rs — /think command
- Add `SlashCommand::Think { mode: ThinkingMode }`.
- Parse `/think` → list current mode.
- Parse `/think off` → ThinkingMode::Off.
- Parse `/think low` → ThinkingMode::Low.
- Parse `/think high` → ThinkingMode::High.
- Call a new `GrokAcpAgent::set_thinking_mode(session_id, mode)` method.
- Return a `BuiltinResult::Handled(msg)` describing the change.

### 7. src/acp/mod.rs — set_thinking_mode()
- Add `pub async fn set_thinking_mode(&self, session_id: &SessionId, mode: ThinkingMode) -> Result<()>`.

### 8. src/main.rs / CLI chat command
- Add `--thinking <off|low|high>` flag to the `chat` subcommand.
- Pass it through `SessionConfig` when starting a chat session.

### 9. Config files + docs
- Add `thinking_mode = "off"` to `[acp]` in `config.example.toml` with a comment.
- Document in `CONFIGURATION.md`.
- Add CHANGELOG entry.

## Network / Starlink Considerations
Thinking mode requests take significantly longer (especially `high`).  Ensure:
- `timeout_secs` is respected (already configured for long operations).
- The existing Starlink retry loop covers thinking-mode requests without special changes (they're the same HTTP call, just longer).

subtasks.title: Add ThinkingMode enum + AcpConfig.thinking_mode + SessionConfig.thinking_mode
Add reasoning_effort to grok_api builder (../grok_crate/grok_api/src/client.rs)
Wire thinking_mode through GrokClient::chat_completion_with_history
Parse thinking_content from API response; surface in handle_chat_completion
Add /think slash command + set_thinking_mode() agent method
Add --thinking CLI flag to the chat subcommand
Update config.example.toml, CONFIGURATION.md, and CHANGELOG.md

tasks.testStrategy:
1. `cargo build --release` and `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` pass.
2. Unit test: `ThinkingMode` serialises to/from `"off"`, `"low"`, `"high"` correctly.
3. Unit test: `SessionConfig` default has `thinking_mode = ThinkingMode::Off`.
4. Integration test (with GROK_API_KEY): start session, send `/think high`, verify session config updated, make a simple request, verify response includes a `<details>` block with thinking content (or gracefully skips it if the model returns none).
5. Slash command test: parse `/think low` → produces `SlashCommand::Think { mode: ThinkingMode::Low }`.
6. `--thinking high` CLI flag sets `SessionConfig::thinking_mode` before first call.
7. No regression on sessions using `ThinkingMode::Off` (default).
tasks.title: grok-4.3 Thinking Modes — reasoning_effort Support

tasks.description: Replace grok-cli's hand-rolled ACP JSON-RPC implementation with the official agent-client-protocol crate (v0.11+). Three phases: A) research & code audit, B) schema-type swap then connection-layer rewrite, C) adopt all new protocol features the crate unlocks (session resume, elicitation, session fork, MCP-over-ACP bridging).
tasks.details:
## Why migrate?
The official crate (https://crates.io/crates/agent-client-protocol v0.11+) is maintained by the Zed team, powers Zed's own agent integration natively, and has >1.2 M downloads in the last 90 days. grok-cli currently hand-rolls every ACP wire-format type in src/acp/protocol.rs and the entire JSON-RPC dispatch loop in src/cli/commands/acp.rs (~1 800 lines). The multi-word slash-command name bug fixed in May 2026 is a textbook example of what the crate's strongly-typed schema would have caught at compile time. Migrating means every future ACP protocol update becomes a one-line version bump instead of manual archaeology.

## Phase A — Research & Audit (111.1)
Read the crate source + docs.rs, and compare every type in src/acp/protocol.rs against agent_client_protocol::schema::*. Categorise each type as REPLACE / EXTEND / KEEP. Produce Doc/acp-migration-map.md.

## Phase B — Migration (111.2 – 111.4)
111.2  Schema-types swap: add the crate as a real dependency (default-features = false), replace REPLACE types in protocol.rs with re-exports / type aliases, keep grok-specific structs (BayesEngine, ThinkingMode, Goal, etc.).
111.3  Connection-layer rewrite: replace the raw BufReader/BufWriter loop in run_acp_session with the crate's Builder + Agent trait. Map each existing handler (handle_initialize, handle_session_new, handle_session_prompt, etc.) to the corresponding agent callback. Keep all grok custom session logic untouched (Bayesian engine, context archives, goals, thinking mode).
111.4  Slash-command advertisement: replace the hand-rolled AvailableCommand struct with agent_client_protocol::schema::AvailableCommand — verify the crate enforces single-word names at the type level so the class of bug fixed in task 110 can never recur.

## Phase C — New Protocol Features (111.5 – 111.8)
111.5  Session resume (unstable_session_resume): persist full session state to .grok/sessions/<id>.json on close; reload it on session/load. Replaces the current stub that always returns null history.
111.6  Elicitation (unstable_elicitation): replace the ad-hoc PermissionBridge with the official elicitation RPC so Zed / Gemini CLI show structured Allow / Allow Always / Deny dialogs.
111.7  Session fork (unstable_session_fork): clone session state (messages + config + Bayes engine + goal) into a new session ID when a client sends session/fork — enables Zed's branch-conversation feature.
111.8  MCP-over-ACP bridging (agent_client_protocol::mcp_server): expose grok-cli's 32 LLM-callable tools as a standard MCP server endpoint over the ACP transport so any MCP client can discover and invoke them.

subtasks.title: Phase A — Audit crate API; produce Doc/acp-migration-map.md
Phase B-1 — Swap protocol.rs schema types for official crate equivalents
Phase B-2 — Rewrite ACP connection loop using the Agent trait
Phase B-3 — Replace AvailableCommand with official crate type in slash_commands.rs
Phase C-1 — Session resume: persist + reload session state via session/load
Phase C-2 — Elicitation: replace PermissionBridge with official elicitation RPC
Phase C-3 — Session fork: clone session state on session/fork request
Phase C-4 — MCP-over-ACP: expose LLM tools as MCP server endpoint

tasks.testStrategy:
Phase A: Doc/acp-migration-map.md exists with every protocol.rs type classified.
Phase B: after each subtask, cargo test --lib passes all existing tests (45 slash-command + all other lib tests). Integration smoke-test with Zed: /bayes show, /think high, 3-turn chat — behaviour identical to pre-migration. cargo clippy --all-targets zero warnings.
Phase C per feature — 111.5: kill grok mid-session, relaunch, send session/load, confirm history+goal restored. 111.6: trigger a file-write tool, confirm Zed shows structured permission dialog. 111.7: fork conversation in Zed, confirm independent branch. 111.8: connect plain MCP client to ACP stdio, list tools — all 32 visible.
tasks.title: Migrate ACP Layer to Official agent-client-protocol Rust Crate

tasks.description: Design and implement a Text-Graph Synergy RAG engine for grok-cli using a hybrid tree-sitter + syn parser to build a semantic-entity project graph (structs, enums, traits, impls, functions, modules), coupled with embeddings and hybrid retrieval (BM25 + vectors) to drive graph-aware, retrieval-aware context compression and LLM prompting.
tasks.details:
## Background
Current grok-cli context handling includes context discovery and AI-powered summarization/archiving, but it is linear and text-only. The next step is a Text-Graph Synergy RAG (TGS-RAG) engine that couples a semantic project graph with embeddings and hybrid retrieval. The goal is to retrieve minimal, highly relevant context for each request by traversing a semantic entity graph (structs, enums, traits, impls, functions, modules) and combining it with vector search and BM25.

This epic uses a hybrid parsing approach: tree-sitter for fast, incremental file/function boundaries and syn for deep semantic extraction. The resulting graph will power graph-expanded retrieval, reranking, and Context Compression 2.0.

## Scope
- Build a semantic-entity project graph for Rust (and later, language-agnostic hooks).
- Integrate embeddings + BM25 hybrid retrieval.
- Implement graph-expanded retrieval and reranking.
- Implement Context Compression 2.0 (retrieval-aware pruning + summarization).
- Integrate with ACP sessions, Session DNA, and existing context-discovery pipeline.
- Add configuration, docs, and tests.

## Out of Scope
- Non-Rust language-specific parsers (future tasks).
- Full MCP-based external knowledge sources (future tasks).

subtasks.details: Created Doc/TGS_RAG_ARCHITECTURE.md and initial src/rag/ module with GraphNode, GraphEdge, ProjectGraph, and supporting types.
subtasks.title: Design TGS-RAG architecture and data model (semantic entity graph + retrieval pipeline)

subtasks.details: Added optional dependencies behind 'tgs-rag' feature in Cargo.toml.
subtasks.title: Add tree-sitter-rust + syn dependencies and feature flags
Implement tree-sitter-based file and function boundary scanner
Implement syn-based semantic entity extractor (structs, enums, traits, impls, functions)
Define GraphNode and GraphEdge types for semantic entity graph
Build project graph builder: scan workspace, parse files, populate semantic entity graph
Add graph persistence and incremental update mechanism
Integrate embeddings index (vectors) for graph nodes + BM25 text index
Implement hybrid retrieval: BM25 + embeddings over graph nodes
Implement graph-expanded retrieval (neighbors, parents, children, related entities)
Implement reranking layer for retrieved nodes and chunks
Implement Context Compression 2.0: retrieval-aware pruning + summarization
Wire TGS-RAG into ACP session context assembly pipeline
Integrate Session DNA and project context discovery with TGS-RAG
Add configuration options for TGS-RAG (enable/disable, budgets, thresholds)
Add logging and debug tooling for TGS-RAG decisions (selected nodes, edges, chunks)
Add unit tests for graph builder and retrieval components
Add integration tests for TGS-RAG in real Rust projects
Update config.example.toml, CONFIGURATION.md, and README.md with TGS-RAG details
Add CHANGELOG entry for TGS-RAG and Context Compression 2.0

tasks.testStrategy:
1. `cargo build --release` and `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` pass after each subtask.
2. Unit tests for graph construction: given a small Rust fixture project, the graph contains expected nodes (structs, enums, traits, impls, functions) and edges (module imports, call edges, impl relationships).
3. Unit tests for retrieval: given a query about a specific function or struct, TGS-RAG returns the correct primary node plus relevant neighbors.
4. Integration test: run grok-cli in a Rust project, ask for a refactor of a function that touches multiple modules; verify only relevant files/functions are loaded into context and the patch is consistent with the graph.
5. Regression test: context compression still triggers correctly when context is full, but now uses graph-aware pruning.
6. Logging/telemetry: debug logs show which graph nodes and chunks were selected for a given request.
7. Performance: indexing a medium-sized Rust project (< 1k files) completes within a reasonable time (e.g., < 5 seconds on a typical dev machine).
tasks.title: TGS-RAG Epic — Text-Graph Synergy Retrieval Engine (Semantic Entity Graph)

tasks.description: Add a token caching layer to Grok-CLI that stores system prompt hashes, tool schema fingerprints, compressed context, and tokenized segments to reduce repeated prompt construction.
tasks.details: Create src/optimizer/token_cache.rs with structs for TokenCache and methods for storing and retrieving cached prompt components.

subtasks.title: Define TokenCache struct
Implement system prompt hashing
Implement tool schema fingerprinting
Add compressed context caching
Integrate cache into prompt builder

tasks.testStrategy: Simulate repeated requests and verify that cached components are reused and token count decreases by at least 30%.
tasks.title: Implement Token Cache Module

tasks.description: Add a cost-aware routing layer that selects model, compression level, and token budget based on request type and complexity.
tasks.details: Create src/optimizer/cost_router.rs with CostProfile and routing logic for cheap, balanced, and high-precision modes.

subtasks.title: Define CostProfile struct
Implement request classification
Add model selection logic
Integrate router into session pipeline

tasks.testStrategy: Verify that different request types trigger different cost profiles and that token usage aligns with configured budgets.

tasks.description: Add delta-based prompt construction that sends only changed messages, updated tool schemas, and new context segments.

Implement diff computation
Integrate delta builder into prompt pipeline

tasks.testStrategy: Verify that repeated requests send only deltas and reduce token usage by 40–70%.

tasks.description: Move create_capabilities() and get_available_tool_definitions() out of GrokAcpAgent::new() so Zed ACP stdio starts faster. Compute capabilities lazily on first initialize/session.new.
tasks.details: create_capabilities() currently rebuilds 30+ tool JSON schemas on every ACP launch. Defer this until the first real request from Zed.
tasks.testStrategy: Measure time from 'grok acp stdio' to ready state before/after. Verify capabilities still appear correctly in initialize response.
tasks.title: Lazy-load ACP capabilities and tool definitions

tasks.description: Wrap get_tool_definitions() and get_available_tool_definitions() results in a global lazy static so the expensive JSON construction only happens once per process.
tasks.details: The big vec![json!(...)] in registry.rs is rebuilt on every agent creation. Cache it.
tasks.testStrategy: Add a simple timing test or log line showing construction only happens once.
tasks.title: Cache tool definitions globally with once_cell / LazyLock

tasks.description: Push AppRouter construction even later — only on first actual chat completion, not during agent creation.
tasks.details: Currently done in new() when an API key exists. Defer to first handle_chat_completion call.
tasks.testStrategy: Confirm no API key validation or network work happens until first prompt in Zed.
tasks.title: Make AppRouter creation fully lazy in ACP path

tasks.description: Add a fast-path flag that skips non-essential init (knowledge packs, SessionDNA, Bayesian engine, HookManager) until after the first prompt when launched from Zed.
tasks.details: Zed starts 90% of ACP sessions. A dedicated fast path can shave noticeable time.
tasks.testStrategy: Benchmark with and without the flag. Ensure full features still activate on first real prompt.
tasks.title: Add --zed / --fast startup flag for ACP stdio

tasks.description: Run a startup profiler (e.g. cargo flamegraph or simple timing) on the ACP stdio entry point and remove or defer anything not required before the first initialize message.
tasks.details: Look at SecurityManager, current_dir canonicalization, tracing setup, and chat_logger initialization.
tasks.testStrategy: Produce before/after timing numbers for 'grok acp stdio' and actual Zed connection.
tasks.title: Profile and trim unnecessary work in start_acp_stdio path

tasks.description: Defer creation of SecurityManager and HookManager until they are actually needed instead of doing it in every GrokAcpAgent::new().
tasks.details: These are created unconditionally even though many ACP sessions may never need them.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify no behavior change for normal Zed usage.
tasks.title: Make SecurityManager and HookManager initialization lazy

tasks.description: Advance sub-agent and multi-agent capabilities: integrate spawn_agent into the ReasoningEngine + TaskGraph, add AgentManager for lifecycle tracking, improve inter-agent messaging, and expose agent orchestration primitives.

tasks.description: Send real-time notifications to Zed (and other ACP clients) when sub-agents are spawned, forked, joined, or cancelled so the editor can display agent activity.
